arts.ttl: Chelsea Predicted Lineup vs. Southampton: Premier League
arts.sum: Chelsea looks to keep the pace with Arsenal and close the gap to Liverpool in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Chelsea make huge Mykhailo Mudryk transfer decision as Enzo Maresca reveals Todd Boehly meeting
arts.sum: Despite that, possible incomings will have likely been discussed by Maresca with Chelsea owners Todd Boehly and Behdad Eghbali. Chelsea are absolutely flying in the Premier League under Enzo Maresca and the temptation to recruit in January may be strong.

arts.ttl: Southampton vs Chelsea: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Chelsea travel to Southampton in the Premier League on Wednesday night. Preview includes how to watch on TV, team news, predicted lineups and score prediction.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca sets timeline for Chelsea to 'dominate' the Premier League
arts.sum: Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca has spoken about his lofty expectations for his expensively assembled squad.

arts.ttl: Chelsea can dominate in next five to 10 years - Maresca
arts.sum: Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca says his side will become a dominant force in the Premier League within the next five to 10 years.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca bullish on Chelsea’s future but reins in title talk for now
arts.sum: Manager has led Chelsea to third place in Premier League ‘In the next five to ten years, Chelsea can dominate’ For Enzo Maresca, there is a difference. Does he worry? Of course, he does. He is the manager of Chelsea and it tends to come with the territory. Does he doubt himself? Absolutely not. When Maresca was appointed six months ago, 44 years old and with little managerial experience, the reaction was predictable. It will not work. He will not work. Not at Chelsea. Not with these owners and this enormous squad. Maresca saw it differently, partly because of these owners and this enormous squad, in which the talent runs so deep. But mainly because of his self-belief. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca backs his Chelsea side to 'dominate English football'... but insists the Blues are not ready to be the top dogs in the Premier League yet
arts.sum: Tuesday was Maresca's six-month anniversary of his appointment. He had considerable doubters at the time, some even saying he would be out of Chelsea by December.
Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca has no doubts about the quality of his players, but the Italian thinks this season is too early to talk about winning the Premier League title.
